buisinessVisitorVisaSubHead: Business Visitors in Canada: Meetings, Events, and Conferences.
buisinessVisitorVisaPara: Business Visa is a great option for business owners who want to travel to Canada for purposes of exploring business opportunities. The best part about this program is that you do not require any sponsorship.
ActivitiesHeading: As a business visitor, you may engage in the following activities:
a1: Purchasing Canadian goods or services for a foreign business or government.
a2: Accepting orders for goods or services.
a3: Attending meetings, conferences, conventions, or trade fairs.
a4: Providing after-sales service related to warranties or sales agreements.
a5: Receiving training from a Canadian parent company (while employed outside Canada).
a6: Training employees of a Canadian branch of a foreign company.
a7: Receiving training from a Canadian company that sold you equipment or services.
ActivitiesSubPara: Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA): U.S. or Mexican nationals may participate in other activities such as research, marketing, and general services. Refer to the Global Affairs Canada website for details.
EligibilityHeading: Eligibility criteria for a Business Visitor Visa?
EligibilityPara: Before proceeding, learn whether you qualify as a business visitor or if you need to apply for a work permit instead. Business visitors typically stay in Canada for a few days or weeks to attend meetings or events, with a maximum stay of up to 6 months. A separate application can be filed to extend the stay.
EligLiHead: To qualify as a business visitor, you must meet the following requirements:
e1: If you require a business visitor visa for Canada and your meeting, event, or conference is registered with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC), your organizer will provide an event code.
e2: Include this event code on your visa application form to indicate your participation in a registered event.
e3: Business Ties Outside Canada: Your primary business operations, income, and profits are outside Canada.
e4: Documentary Support:You possess documents supporting your application.
e5: Basic Entry Requirements: You meet Canada’s basic entry criteria, including:
e5SubLi2: Adequate funds for your stay and return
e5SubLi3: Intent to leave Canada at the end of your stay
e5SubLi4: No criminal, security, or health risks to Canadians
EligLastPara: If your stay exceeds 6 months or involves working in Canada, you may be considered a temporary worker and need to apply for a work permit.
EssenDocuPara: Upon arrival at the border, ensure you have the following documents:
ed1: Valid passport or travel document for the entire stay.
ed3: Same passport used in your eTA application (if applicable).
ed4: For U.S. lawful permanent residents (green card holders), a valid green card and passport from your country of nationality (or equivalent document).
ed5: Letters of support from your parent company.
ed6: Letter of invitation from the Canadian host business or recognition letter from the Canada Border Services Agency.
ed7: Relevant documents such as warranty or service agreements.
ed8: 24-hour contact details of your business host in Canada.
ed9: Proof of sufficient funds to cover your stay and return journey.

q1: How long can you stay in Canada on a Business Visitor Visa?
qa1: Your planned stay should be less than 6 months.
q2: What activities am I allowed to do on a Business Visitor Visa trip?
qa2:
As a business visitor, you may engage in the following activities:
  • Purchasing Canadian goods or services for a foreign business or government.
  • Accepting orders for goods or services.
  • Attending meetings, conferences, conventions, or trade fairs.
  • Providing after-sales service related to warranties or sales agreements.
  • Receiving training from a Canadian parent company (while employed outside Canada).
  • Training employees of a Canadian branch of a foreign company.
  • Receiving training from a Canadian company that sold you equipment or services.
q3: What is an even code for a Business Visitor Visa?
qa3: If you require a business visitor visa for Canada and your meeting, event, or conference is registered with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC), your organizer will provide an event code. Include this event code on your visa application form to indicate your participation in a registered event.
q4: Can I purchase goods for my foreign business on my trip to Business Visitor Visa trip to Canada?
qa4: Yes, as a business visitor, you are allowed to purchase Canadian goods or services for a foreign business.
q5: I bought Equipment and services while I was on a Business Visitor Visa trip, am I allowed to get training on this equipment and services I purchased?
qa5: Yes, as a business visitor, receiving training from a Canadian company that sold you equipment or services is permitted.
q6: I got orders for my business while I was on a Business Visitor Visa trip, can I accept these orders?
qa6: Yes, you are allowed to Accept orders for goods or services, while you travel to Canada on a Business Visitor Visa.
